warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) and most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites for benzaldehyde; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF  (backend invoked via chemkit).  
Geometry provenance: fixed, single-point evaluation on the supplied XYZ geometry `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz`; no geometry optimisation performed (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1); open-shell doublets were used for the N + 1 and N – 1 states (cation charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion charge –1, multiplicity 2) to construct the finite-difference Fukui functions.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF (DFT) under the hood.
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for the neutral, cation, and anion all converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.  The engine emitted three warnings (verbatim below) concerning charge conservation and a schema header — see below.
Live log: engine stdout/SCF progress was streamed to a `.out` file in the working directory during the run (path surfaced live when the job was launched).

Headline result
The most electrophilic atom is C3 (index 2, the carbonyl carbon) with f⁺ = 0.39938962.

Warnings (verbatim)
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

The computation is therefore classified as computed (not experimental).
